prd.oneLiner: Connect accounts, costs, tags, budgets, anomalous spend, approvals, savings actions, and reporting in one operating flow.
prd.goal: Reduce cloud spend anomaly detection time and achieve 95% or more tag-based cost allocation coverage.
prd.whyNow: Cloud costs, budgets, owners, and savings evidence are scattered across accounts and tools, making overspend causes and accountability hard to identify quickly.
prd.problem: Cloud costs, budgets, owners, and savings evidence are scattered across accounts and tools, making overspend causes and accountability hard to identify quickly.
prd.solution: Connect accounts, costs, tags, budgets, anomalous spend, approvals, savings actions, and reporting in one operating flow.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Engineering and FinOps team managing cloud budgets and usage
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Cloud costs, budgets, owners, and savings evidence are scattered across accounts and tools, making overspend causes and accountability hard to identify quickly.

targets.name: FinOps and cloud operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Cloud costs, budgets, owners, and savings evidence are scattered across accounts and tools, making overspend causes and accountability hard to identify quickly.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Cloud costs, budgets, owners, and savings evidence are scattered across accounts and tools, making overspend causes and accountability hard to identify quickly.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Reduce cloud spend anomaly detection time and achieve 95% or more tag-based cost allocation coverage.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Cloud account, budget, and cost-policy registration
Cost, usage, budget, tag, and spend-anomaly lookup
Cost allocation, budget approval, anomaly detection, and savings-action handling
Cost trend, budget consumption, savings impact, and anomalous-spend reporting
Cloud account, budget, and cost-policy registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
